faqs.question: What are drain flies and how do I know if I have them in my Woodhaven home?
faqs.answer: Drain flies are small, fuzzy-winged insects that breed in the organic matter inside drains and pipes. You'll typically spot them near sinks, showers, or floor drains, especially in bathrooms and basements. They're weak fliers and often appear in clusters on walls near drains. If you see small moth-like insects hovering around your drains in Woodhaven, you likely have a drain fly infestation.

faqs.question: How much does drain fly treatment cost in Woodhaven?
faqs.answer: Our drain fly treatment in Woodhaven ranges from $350 to $550, depending on the severity of the infestation and the number of drains affected. This includes inspection, treatment of all affected drains, application of bio-enzymatic cleaners, and a follow-up visit if needed. We provide free estimates and transparent pricing with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: How long does it take to eliminate drain flies from my Woodhaven property?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments show significant results within 3-5 days, with complete elimination typically achieved within 1-2 weeks. The timeline depends on the extent of the infestation and the number of breeding sites. Our treatment includes removing the organic buildup where larvae develop, so adult flies will die off naturally once breeding stops. We offer follow-up visits to ensure complete eradication.

faqs.question: Can I prevent drain flies from coming back after treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes! After our professional treatment, we recommend regular drain maintenance including weekly flushing with hot water, monthly use of bio-enzymatic drain cleaners, and keeping drains covered when not in use. In Woodhaven's older buildings, we can also advise on plumbing improvements and moisture control to prevent future infestations. Our technicians will provide customized prevention tips based on your specific property.
